title: Xenial Uruguayan Gruyere Flatbread
description: A briny, shaggy, comforting flatbread packed with Gruyere cheese, perfect for a quick lunch on a tava-griddle.
instructions: Combine flour, salt, sugar and yeast in a large bowl.
Add warm water and olive oil and stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for 5 minutes until smooth.
Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 30 minutes until doubled.
Punch down the dough and divide it into two equal portions.
Roll each portion into a flat disc about 0.5cm thick.
Sprinkle grated Gruyere, oregano, paprika and thyme evenly over the discs.
Press the toppings lightly into the dough with your fingers.
Heat a tava-griddle over medium heat and cook each flatbread for 4 minutes per side until golden and bubbly.
Serve warm straight from the griddle for a comforting, briny bite.
